blocks.spoken: So, uh, platform services are different in the case that, well, they need to operate sometimes outside the instances. Uh, we have a integration management, which is for the App Store and apps, and we have like a settings management, right? And these are the services that will for for example, save some of the data without any isolation at all. And that's okay because they are platform services and they can work that way, right?

blocks.spoken: Um, right, so let's talk about the isolation levels that you might see on the business and platform services because like this is mainly the the the distinction between business and platforms. So what the isolation level means is it's it's sort of configuration that you set on your table so then component recognize what kind of what kind of isolation needs to be applied on your table. And as I said, most of our tables will be instance isolated. So we will always have the tenant and instance for our data because we we for the most part, we are very interested in which customer and in which environment of the customer, we should save that data and then we should read and edit and then delete it.

segment_summary: This segment explains the difference between business and platform services, highlighting how platform services operate outside instances and require different isolation levels. The speaker then details various isolation levels (Global, Tenant, Instance) and introduces the 'Master instance' concept, which allows for a base set of data with customer-specific additions. Finally, the segment transitions to a demo, showing the current state of a `TemplateManagementDbContext` without isolation and confirming no pending database migrations before proceeding with the implementation of the isolation component.
